---
title: "09: Data Frames (Solutions)"
author: "Charlie Lindgren"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
```

## Solutions

```{r, eval=TRUE, echo=TRUE}
# Solution to Exercise 1
data(mtcars)
head(mtcars)

# Solution to Exercise 2
select(mtcars, mpg, cyl, hp)

# Solution to Exercise 3
filter(mtcars, cyl > 6)

# Solution to Exercise 4
mutate(mtcars, hp_per_cyl = hp / cyl)

# Solution to Exercise 5
arrange(mtcars, desc(mpg))

# Solution to Exercise 6
mtcars %>%
  group_by(cyl) %>%
  summarise(avg_mpg = mean(mpg))

# Solution to Exercise 7
empty_df <- data.frame()
print(empty_df)

# Solution to Exercise 8
students_df <- data.frame(
  id = c(1, 2, 3),
  name = c("Alice", "Bob", "Charlie"),
  score = c(85, 92, 78)
)
print(students_df)

# Solution to Exercise 9
str(students_df)
summary(students_df)

# Solution to Exercise 10
students_df$name

# Solution to Exercise 11
students_df[1:2, ]

# Solution to Exercise 12
students_df[3, c("name", "score")]

# Solution to Exercise 13
students_df <- students_df %>%
  mutate(grade = case_when(
    score >= 90 ~ "A",
    score >= 80 ~ "B",
    TRUE ~ "C"
  ))
print(students_df)

# Solution to Exercise 14
students_df <- students_df %>%
  select(-score)
print(students_df)

# Solution to Exercise 15
students_df <- students_df %>%
  rename(student_name = name)
print(students_df)

# Solution to Exercise 16
courses_df <- data.frame(
  id = c(1, 2, 3),
  course = c("Math", "Science", "History")
)
merged_df <- left_join(students_df, courses_df, by = "id")
print(merged_df)

# Solution to Exercise 17
df1 <- data.frame(A = 1:2, B = c("X", "Y"))
df2 <- data.frame(A = 3:4, B = c("Z", "W"))
combined_rows_df <- bind_rows(df1, df2)
print(combined_rows_df)

# Solution to Exercise 18
df3 <- data.frame(id = 1:2, C = c(10, 20))
df4 <- data.frame(id = 1:2, D = c("P", "Q"))
combined_cols_df <- bind_cols(df3, df4)
print(combined_cols_df)

# Solution to Exercise 19
mtcars_long <- mtcars %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(mpg, disp, hp),
    names_to = "metric",
    values_to = "value"
  )
print(head(mtcars_long))

# Solution to Exercise 20
mean(mtcars$mpg)

# Solution to Exercise 21
nrow(mtcars)
ncol(mtcars)

# Solution to Exercise 22
colMeans(mtcars)

# Solution to Exercise 23
rowSums(mtcars)
```
